title: taraxacum dolmiticum
intro: Taraxacum dolmiticum, also known as the Dinaric dandelion or Dinaric hawk's beard, is a perennial plant species within the Taraxacum genus, closely related to the more well-known dandelion (Taraxacum officinale). The medicinal properties of Taraxacum dolmiticum are attributed to its roots and leaves, which contain inulin, taraxasterol, and other bioactive compounds. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as potential uses in the treatment of digestive issues and skin conditions. Taraxacum dolmiticum is often prepared for medicinal use in the form of infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
